issues: Jason Phillips won tournament MVP, not Frank Romeo. The OCR states Phillips 'was selected the tournament's [MVP]' (sentence cuts off but clearly refers to Phillips). Frank Romeo is described as winning 'major individual awards' for goaltending, which aligns with the known Centennial Cup MVP being Frank Romeo — but the OCR attributes the tournament selection to Phillips. Per archive key facts, Frank Romeo was the Centennial Cup MVP, so the draft's claim that 'Jason Phillips... earned tournament MVP honors' is incorrect.

The draft incorrectly states the Sockeyes 'captured five titles including the Centennial Cup' but names only five correctly per the OCR: BCJHL, Mowat Cup, Alberta-BC, Abbott Cup, and Centennial Cup — this part is actually consistent with the OCR.

The headline in the draft includes '& CANADIAN PRESS' noise character at the start. The actual headline text from OCR (as a photo caption/wire credit line) is 'CHAMPAGNE SHOWER anoints Richmond Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach' — the '&' is OCR noise and 'CANADIAN PRESS' is the wire photo credit, not part of the headline.

The byline is listed as 'Elliott Pap' in the draft but the OCR clearly reads 'ELLIOTT PAP Sun Sports Reporter', so the capitalization/formatting is close but the draft omits his title. The name itself is correct.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 5-2 to win the Centennial Cup, Canada's tier-two junior hockey championship, in Humboldt, Saskatchewan.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Coach Orland Kurtenbach's Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 5-2 Saturday night in Humboldt, Saskatchewan to clinch the Centennial Cup, Canada's tier-two junior hockey championship. The victory capped a remarkable playoff run in which the Sockeyes won 27 of 34 games and captured five titles: the BC Junior League, Mowat Cup, Alberta-BC, Abbott Cup (western Canada), and the Centennial Cup. Jason Phillips capped a fantastic week with a hat trick in the final game and was selected tournament MVP, while goaltender Frank Romeo also took home major individual honours. Elliott Pap of the Vancouver Sun reported from Humboldt as the team prepared to bring the Cup home to Richmond.
review.correctedHeadline: CHAMPAGNE SHOWER anoints Richmond Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach
review.notes: The OCR sentence about tournament MVP is cut off mid-sentence ("was selected the tournament's") but context strongly implies Phillips was named tournament MVP. Archive key facts state Frank Romeo was Centennial Cup MVP — this conflicts with the OCR. Editor should verify: it's possible Phillips won a different tournament award (e.g. top scorer) and Romeo won MVP, or the OCR truncation is misleading. The '& CANADIAN PRESS' prefix on the headline is a wire photo credit line for the champagne-shower photo, not part of the article headline.
